contents.value:
```javascript
interface Console
```
The `console` object provides access to the debugging console (e.g. the Web console in browsers or stdout/stderr in Node.js). It is accessible via the global `console` object.

```javascript
console.log(...data: any[]): void
```
Outputs a message to the console. Supports string substitution and additional arguments.

```javascript
console.error(...data: any[]): void
```
Outputs an error message to the console. In browsers, the message is styled as an error.

```javascript
console.warn(...data: any[]): void
```
Outputs a warning message to the console. In browsers, displayed with a yellow warning icon.

```javascript
console.info(...data: any[]): void
```
Outputs an informational message to the console.

```javascript
console.table(tabularData: any, properties?: string[]): void
```
Displays tabular data as a table. Takes an array or object and an optional array of column names to include.

```javascript
console.dir(item: any, options?: object): void
```
Displays an interactive list of the properties of the specified JavaScript object.

```javascript
console.time(label?: string): void
```
Starts a timer with a given label. Call `console.timeEnd()` with the same label to stop and print the elapsed time.

```javascript
console.timeEnd(label?: string): void
```
Stops a timer previously started by `console.time()` and prints the elapsed time.

```javascript
console.assert(condition: boolean, ...data: any[]): void
```
Writes an error message to the console if the assertion is false. If true, nothing happens.

```javascript
namespace JSON
```
The `JSON` object contains methods for parsing JSON and converting values to JSON. It cannot be called or constructed.

```javascript
JSON.parse(text: string, reviver?: (key: string, value: any) => any): any
```
Parses a JSON string, constructing the JavaScript value or object described by the string.

**Throws** `SyntaxError` if the string to parse is not valid JSON.

```javascript
JSON.stringify(
  value: any,
  replacer?: (key: string, value: any) => any | (string | number)[] | null,
  space?: string | number
): string | undefined
```
Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string. The `replacer` function or array controls which properties are included. The `space` parameter controls indentation.

```javascript
Array.from<T>(arrayLike: ArrayLike<T> | Iterable<T>, mapFn?: (v: T, k: number) => U, thisArg?: any): U[]
```
Creates a new, shallow-copied Array instance from an iterable or array-like object. Optionally maps each element through `mapFn`.

```javascript
Array.isArray(value: any): value is any[]
```
Determines whether the passed value is an `Array`. Returns `true` for arrays, `false` otherwise.

```javascript
Array<T>.map<U>(callbackfn: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U, thisArg?: any): U[]
```
Creates a new array populated with the results of calling the provided function on every element in the calling array.

```javascript
Array<T>.filter(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): T[]
```
Creates a shallow copy of a portion of an array, filtered to only include elements where the predicate returns true.

```javascript
Array<T>.reduce<U>(callbackfn: (accumulator: U, currentValue: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U, initialValue: U): U
```
Executes a user-supplied reducer callback function on each element of the array, passing in the return value of the calculation on the preceding element.

```javascript
Array<T>.forEach(callbackfn: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => void, thisArg?: any): void
```
Executes a provided function once for each array element. Returns `undefined`. Cannot be stopped (use `for...of` or `for` instead).

```javascript
Array<T>.find(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): T | undefined
```
Returns the first element that satisfies the testing function. Returns `undefined` if no element is found.

```javascript
Array<T>.findIndex(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): number
```
Returns the index of the first element that satisfies the testing function. Returns `-1` if none found.

```javascript
Array<T>.some(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): boolean
```
Tests whether at least one element in the array passes the test implemented by the provided function.

```javascript
Array<T>.every(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): boolean
```
Tests whether all elements in the array pass the test implemented by the provided function.

```javascript
Array<T>.flat(depth?: number): FlatArray<T, number>[]
```
Creates a new array with all sub-array elements concatenated into it recursively up to the specified depth (default: 1).

```javascript
Array<T>.flatMap<U>(callback: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U | U[], thisArg?: any): U[]
```
Maps each element using a mapping function, then flattens the result into a new array (equivalent to `map().flat(1)`).

```javascript
Array<T>.includes(searchElement: T, fromIndex?: number): boolean
```
Determines whether an array includes a certain value, returning `true` or `false`. Uses SameValueZero comparison.

```javascript
Array<T>.at(index: number): T | undefined
```
Returns the element at the given index. Accepts negative integers which count back from the last item.

```javascript
Object.keys(obj: object): string[]
```
Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property names.

```javascript
Object.values(obj: object): any[]
```
Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property values.

```javascript
Object.entries(obj: object): [string, any][]
```
Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property `[key, value]` pairs.

```javascript
Object.assign(target: object, ...sources: object[]): object
```
Copies all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object. Returns the modified target object.

```javascript
Object.freeze<T>(obj: T): Readonly<T>
```
Freezes an object: other code cannot delete or change its properties. Note: this is shallow — nested objects are not frozen.

```javascript
Object.fromEntries(iterable: Iterable<[string, any]>): object
```
Transforms a list of key-value pairs into an object.

```javascript
Object.groupBy<T>(items: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (element: T, index: number) => string): Record<string, T[]>
```
*ES2024* — Groups elements of an iterable according to the string values returned by a callback function.

```javascript
class Promise<T>
```
The `Promise` object represents the eventual completion (or failure) of an asynchronous operation and its resulting value.

States: `pending` → `fulfilled` or `rejected`

```javascript
Promise.all<T>(values: Iterable<T | PromiseLike<T>>): Promise<Awaited<T>[]>
```
Fulfills when all promises fulfill; rejects when any promise rejects. Short-circuits on first rejection.

```javascript
Promise.allSettled<T>(values: Iterable<T | PromiseLike<T>>): Promise<PromiseSettledResult<Awaited<T>>[]>
```
Fulfills when all promises have settled (fulfilled or rejected). Each result has a `status` property ('fulfilled' or 'rejected').

```javascript
Promise.race<T>(values: Iterable<T | PromiseLike<T>>): Promise<Awaited<T>>
```
Settles as soon as any of the promises settles (fulfills or rejects).

```javascript
Promise.any<T>(values: Iterable<T | PromiseLike<T>>): Promise<Awaited<T>>
```
Resolves as soon as any of the promises fulfills. If all reject, rejects with an `AggregateError`.

```javascript
fetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response>
```
Starts the process of fetching a resource from the network, returning a promise that resolves with the `Response` object.

**RequestInit options:** `method`, `headers`, `body`, `signal`, `mode`, `credentials`, `cache`, `redirect`

```javascript
setTimeout(callback: (...args: any[]) => void, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number
```
Sets a timer which executes a function after the specified delay in milliseconds. Returns a timeout ID for use with `clearTimeout()`.

```javascript
setInterval(callback: (...args: any[]) => void, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number
```
Repeatedly calls a function with a fixed time delay between each call. Returns an interval ID for use with `clearInterval()`.

```javascript
parseInt(string: string, radix?: number): number
```
Parses a string argument and returns an integer of the specified radix (base). Returns `NaN` if the first non-whitespace character cannot be converted.

```javascript
parseFloat(string: string): number
```
Parses a string and returns a floating-point number. Returns `NaN` if the string does not start with a number.

```javascript
class Map<K, V>
```
The `Map` object holds key-value pairs and remembers the original insertion order of the keys. Any value (both objects and primitives) may be used as either a key or a value.

**Methods:** `set(key, value)`, `get(key)`, `has(key)`, `delete(key)`, `clear()`, `forEach()`, `entries()`, `keys()`, `values()`

**Property:** `size`

```javascript
class Set<T>
```
The `Set` object lets you store unique values of any type, whether primitive values or object references.

**Methods:** `add(value)`, `has(value)`, `delete(value)`, `clear()`, `forEach()`, `entries()`, `keys()`, `values()`

**Property:** `size`

```javascript
class WeakMap<K extends object, V>
```
A collection of key/value pairs whose keys must be objects or non-registered symbols, held weakly (allow garbage collection).

**Methods:** `set(key, value)`, `get(key)`, `has(key)`, `delete(key)`

```javascript
class WeakSet<T extends object>
```
A collection of garbage-collectable values (objects and non-registered symbols).

**Methods:** `add(value)`, `has(value)`, `delete(value)`

```javascript
function Symbol(description?: string): symbol
```
Creates a new unique Symbol value. Each symbol is immutable and unique.

**Well-known symbols:** `Symbol.iterator`, `Symbol.asyncIterator`, `Symbol.hasInstance`, `Symbol.toPrimitive`, `Symbol.toStringTag`, `Symbol.dispose`, `Symbol.asyncDispose`

```javascript
class Proxy<T extends object>
  constructor(target: T, handler: ProxyHandler<T>)
```
Enables creation of a proxy for another object, which can intercept and redefine fundamental operations.

**Handler traps:** `get`, `set`, `has`, `deleteProperty`, `apply`, `construct`, `ownKeys`, `getPrototypeOf`, etc.

```javascript
namespace Reflect
```
Provides static methods for interceptable JavaScript operations. Methods are the same as those of `Proxy` handler traps.

**Methods:** `Reflect.get()`, `Reflect.set()`, `Reflect.has()`, `Reflect.deleteProperty()`, `Reflect.apply()`, `Reflect.construct()`, `Reflect.ownKeys()`

```javascript
structuredClone<T>(value: T, options?: { transfer: Transferable[] }): T
```
Creates a deep clone of a given value using the structured clone algorithm. Handles circular references, Maps, Sets, Dates, RegExps, ArrayBuffers, etc.

```javascript
class AbortController
```
A controller that allows you to abort one or more Web requests.

**Properties:** `signal: AbortSignal`
**Methods:** `abort(reason?: any)`

```js
const ctrl = new AbortController();
fetch(url, { signal: ctrl.signal });
ctrl.abort();
```

```javascript
class URL
  constructor(url: string, base?: string | URL)
```
Creates and returns a URL object representing an absolute or relative URL.

**Properties:** `href`, `origin`, `protocol`, `hostname`, `port`, `pathname`, `search`, `searchParams`, `hash`, `username`, `password`

```javascript
class URLSearchParams
  constructor(init?: string | Record<string, string> | [string, string][])
```
Utility for working with URL query strings.

**Methods:** `get(name)`, `getAll(name)`, `set(name, value)`, `append(name, value)`, `delete(name)`, `has(name)`, `entries()`, `keys()`, `values()`, `toString()`, `sort()`

```javascript
class RegExp
  constructor(pattern: string | RegExp, flags?: string)
```
Used for matching text with a pattern.

**Flags:** `g` (global), `i` (ignoreCase), `m` (multiline), `s` (dotAll), `u` (unicode), `v` (unicodeSets), `d` (hasIndices), `y` (sticky)

**Methods:** `test(string)`, `exec(string)`
**String methods:** `match()`, `matchAll()`, `replace()`, `replaceAll()`, `search()`, `split()`

```javascript
class Error
  constructor(message?: string, options?: { cause?: any })
```
Base class for all error objects.

**Properties:** `message`, `name`, `stack`, `cause`
**Subclasses:** `TypeError`, `RangeError`, `SyntaxError`, `ReferenceError`, `URIError`, `EvalError`, `AggregateError`

```javascript
class Date
  constructor()
  constructor(value: number | string)
  constructor(year: number, month: number, date?: number, hours?: number, minutes?: number, seconds?: number, ms?: number)
```
Represents a single moment in time. Months are 0-indexed.

**Key methods:** `getTime()`, `toISOString()`, `toLocaleDateString()`, `getFullYear()`, `getMonth()`, `getDate()`, `getHours()`, `getMinutes()`
**Static:** `Date.now()`, `Date.parse()`

```javascript
crypto.randomUUID(): string
```
Generates a v4 UUID using a cryptographically secure random number generator.

Returns a string like `'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a5b-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d'`.

```javascript
queueMicrotask(callback: VoidFunction): void
```
Queues a microtask to be executed before control is returned to the browser's event loop.

```javascript
var globalThis: typeof globalThis
```
The global `this` value. Works in all environments: browser (`window`), Node.js (`global`), Web Workers (`self`).

```javascript
class TextEncoder
```
Always encodes to UTF-8.

**Methods:** `encode(input: string): Uint8Array`, `encodeInto(src: string, dest: Uint8Array): { read: number, written: number }`

```javascript
class TextDecoder
  constructor(label?: string, options?: { fatal?: boolean, ignoreBOM?: boolean })
```
Decodes bytes (e.g., Uint8Array) to a string. Default encoding is UTF-8.

**Methods:** `decode(input?: BufferSource, options?: { stream?: boolean }): string`

```javascript
typeof operand
```
Returns a string indicating the type of the unevaluated operand.

| Expression | Result |
|---|---|
| `typeof 42` | `'number'` |
| `typeof 'str'` | `'string'` |
| `typeof true` | `'boolean'` |
| `typeof undefined` | `'undefined'` |
| `typeof null` | `'object'` |
| `typeof {}` | `'object'` |
| `typeof []` | `'object'` |
| `typeof fn` | `'function'` |
| `typeof Symbol()` | `'symbol'` |
| `typeof 1n` | `'bigint'` |

```javascript
object instanceof Constructor
```
Tests whether `Constructor.prototype` appears anywhere in the prototype chain of `object`. Returns `boolean`.

```javascript
class Array<T>
```
The Array object enables storing a collection of multiple items under a single variable name. Provides numerous instance methods for traversing and mutation.

```javascript
class Object
```
The Object type represents one of JavaScript's data types. It is used to store various keyed collections and more complex entities.

```javascript
class String
```
The String object is used to represent and manipulate a sequence of characters.

```javascript
namespace Math
```
A built-in object that has properties and methods for mathematical constants and functions.

```javascript
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```javascript
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```javascript
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```javascript
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```javascript
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```javascript
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
